text:
St Mary's Hospital 

Institute of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Patel  
St Mary's Hospital  
Institute of Neurology, St Mary's Hospital, London W2 1NY  

Dear Dr Patel,  

Emily Carter, DOB: 17-08-1989, Hospital No: C672154 NHS No. 4728395612  
78 Willow Lane, London, W9 3HT 

Reason for letter: Update to Accessible Information Standard (AIS) preferences following transition to adult services. 

Summary: 
- Communication format: Prefers written information in plain English, font size 14, with headings and bullet points. 
- Alternative formats: Email PDF copy of clinic letters in addition to posted hard copy. 
- Verbal communication: Wishes information to be explained slowly with opportunity to summarise back; consent given to a parent to attend and contribute collateral history when present. 
- Named supporter: With the patient’s consent, a parent/carer may receive copies of clinic correspondence and be present for telephone/video consultations when available. 
- Contact method: Non-urgent matters by NHS email; appointments and reminders by SMS and letter. 
- Sensory/processing needs: Provide a brief agenda at the start of appointments and a written action plan at the end. 
- Reasonable adjustments: Offer first-morning appointments where possible to reduce waiting room time. 

Action: We have updated the electronic record to reflect these AIS preferences and will review annually or sooner if notified by the patient. 

Follow-up: Routine adult neurology follow-up in 6 months, or earlier if the patient requests changes to AIS needs. 

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: I carefully read the letter. It focuses exclusively on Accessible Information Standard preferences, communication methods, and reasonable adjustments. There is no mention of seizures, seizure types, seizure control, or any seizure frequency. Therefore, there is no seizure frequency information to extract from this letter.
reference: Create a reasonable NHS letter updating Accessible Information Standard preferences for epilepsy care

flaws.description: No seizure frequency referenced in gold
Row marked as not OK in raw dataset
